prompt: We're about to merge server/src/modules/webhooks/ (repository.ts, service.ts, routes.ts). Review it for backend architecture issues before it lands — point out anything that violates our onion/clean architecture conventions and say specifically where the code should actually live instead.

seeded_findings.description: routes.ts (presentation layer) imports drizzle-orm and the db schema directly and runs SELECT/INSERT queries against webhook_endpoints, bypassing WebhooksRepository entirely.
The duplicate-endpoint-URL check ('existing.some(e => e.url === url)') is business/validation logic living in the route handler instead of a service/use-case.

WebhooksRepository.claimDueRetries embeds a domain rule (exponential backoff calculation, MAX_ATTEMPTS cutoff) inside the repository/infrastructure layer instead of a domain/application rule the repository merely persists.

Repository methods (listEndpoints, recordDelivery, claimDueRetries) return raw Drizzle rows straight out of the infrastructure layer with no row->entity/DTO mapping, so Drizzle's shape leaks into the service and route layers.

service.ts constructs a concrete 'new OpenAI(...)' client directly at module scope instead of obtaining an LLM provider through the DI container/port (container.llm(...)), bypassing the composition root.
seeded_findings.location: service.ts, top-level `const openai = new OpenAI(...)` and summarize()
